INTRODUCTION

ACKNOWLEDGEMENTS & CONTACT

Tumor microenvironment (TME) profoundly influences the response to neoadjuvant chemotherapy and overall survival in high-grade serous ovarian cancer (HGSC). CD8 T-cells and macrophages are considered the key players within HGSC TME, but other immune cells such as dendritic cells (DCs) and B-cells may be as much important for shaping anti-tumor response. Understanding how the interactions between them are affecting the TME is essential for development of novel combination therapies. 

Objectives of the study:
identification of immune cell types influencing key processes within HGSC intratumoral stroma
analysing how immune activity changes with the proximity to other immune cells and identifying cell-type specific contact-dependent differences in activity of key transcriptional pathways

Multiple key cancer signatures are correlated with immune cell types abundance in intratumoral stroma. As expected, these are immune signatures, but also some of the proliferation and signalling signatures.
Macrophages cell type fraction is highly correlated with elevated activity of key immune activation (MHC-I, MHC-II, complement) and pro-inflammatory signatures (IL-6-JAK-STAT3, inflammatory response, IFNg, TNFa), as well as the ones promoting tumor growth and proliferation (p53, IL2-STAT5 and androgen response).

ro-inflammatory signatures (IL-6-JAK-STAT3, inflammatory response, IFNg, TNFa), as well as the ones promoting tumor growth and proliferation (p53, IL2-STAT5 and androgen response). It highlights the known double-role of macrophages in TME where, on the one hand - they elevate anti-tumor immune response and pro-inflammatory environment (M2-like), but on the other - promotes tumor growth and cause immunesuppression (M1-like).
B-cells fraction is correlated with tumor-growth promoting signatures - DNA repair and MYC targets, and negatively correlated with immune response signature, which may suggest their immunosupressive role within TME. Although mild negative correlation with TNFa is also visible.
Proximity to other cell types can change the activity of the identified pathways within cell-type specific deconvoluted transcriptional signal. 
Macrophages pro-inflammatory signatures are significantly elevated in the presence of both CD8 T-cells (IFNg) and DCs (IFNg, IL6-JAK-STAT3), while the ones promoting tumor growth (p53, androgen response, IL2-STAT5, TNFa) are higher when these cell types are less abundant. That suggest that both CD8 T-cells and DCs may promote pro-inflammatory anti-cancer response of macrophages.

, IL2-STAT5, TNFa) are higher when these cell types are less abundant. That suggest that both CD8 T-cells and DCs may promote pro-inflammatory anti-cancer response of macrophages.
B-cells MYC targets signature is elevated with the higher proportion of DCs and lower proportion of macrophages in the neighborhood, suggesting the opposed role of this cell types on pro-cancer activity of B-cells.

1) Research Program in Systems Oncology, Faculty of Medicine, University of Helsinki
2) Faculty of Medicine and Health Technology, Tampere University and TAYS Cancer Centre, Tampere, Finland

MATERIALS & RESULTS

CONCLUSIONS

Integration of highly-multiplexed imaging and spatial transcriptomics reveal contact-dependent differences in immune related transcriptional pathways in ovarian cancer

NCLUSIONS

Integration of highly-multiplexed imaging and spatial transcriptomics reveal contact-dependent differences in immune related transcriptional pathways in ovarian cancer

Iga Niemiec1, Gayani Anandagoda1, Aleksandra Shabanova1, Zhihan Liang1, Ada Junquera1, Erdogan Pekcan Erkan2, Essi Kahelin1,3, Matilda Salko1, Anna Vähärautio1, Ulla-Maija Haltia1,4, Anni Virtanen1,3, Anniina Färkkilä1,4,5

42 post-NACT HGSC tCycIF whole slide images

10-30 ROIs per sample with different CD8 T-cells (CD8+) Dendritic Cells (CD11a+), B-cells (CD20+) and macrophages (IBA1+) colocalization patterns

GeoMx spatial transcriptomics of corresponding regions on adjacent tissue slide 

Each ROI segmented into 2 separately sequenced areas of interest (AOIs) with 100-300 cells: 
tumor
intratumoral stroma

492 stromal AOIs selected for cell type fraction analysis (as stromal regions contains most of immune cells)

Cell type fractions and cell type specific transcriptional profiles in each AOI computed using SpatialDecon deconvolution algorithm and HGSC scRNAseq dataset as a reference.
Single sample gene set enrichment analysis (ssGSEA) scores of 16 key HGSC-related HALLMARK and MHC-I, MHC-II GO:BP signatures have been computed for full and cell type specific transcriptional signal

et enrichment analysis (ssGSEA) scores of 16 key HGSC-related HALLMARK and MHC-I, MHC-II GO:BP signatures have been computed for full and cell type specific transcriptional signal
Cell type groups are defined as AOIs with highest 0.25 percentile of given immune cell type fraction and highest/lowest 0.25 percentile of other cell type fractions
Relations between cell type fractions and activity of key biological processes examined using pearson correlation coefficient scores and pval threshold 0.05.
Differences between mean ssGSEA scores for cell type groups are computed using wilcoxon rank-sum test

Fig. 1: Experimental design: a) tCycIF guided selection of spatial transcriptomics ROIs on adjacent tissue slide and 
b) integration with scRNAseq dataset to perform cell type deconvolution

Fig. 2: immune cell type fractions in stromal and tumor AOIs

Fig. 4: Differences of ssGSEA scores from a) macrophages and b) B-cells deconvoluted cell type specific transcriptomic signal for selected signatures (corr >= 0.25) between AOI groups with highest and lowest abundance of other cell types

B-cells CD8 T-cells macrophages DCs

Macrophages cell type fractions are highly correlated with activity of multiple signatures related to immune functions, proliferation (p53) and signaling pathways (androgen response, IL2-STAT5 and TNFa).

ractions are highly correlated with activity of multiple signatures related to immune functions, proliferation (p53) and signaling pathways (androgen response, IL2-STAT5 and TNFa). B-cells abundance is correlated with DNA repair and proliferation (MYC targets) and, interestingly, negatively correlated with inflammatory response and TNFa signalling. Neither CD8 T-cells nor DCs seems to influence any of the selected signatures.

Fractions of all immune cells are higher within intratumoral stroma of HGSC. Macrophages being the most abundant (mean fraction 0.23), followed by CD8 T-cells (0.15), Bcells (0.08) and DCs (0.05). Macrophages and CD8 T-cells are also the only immune cells infiltrating tumor mass.

Fig. 3: correlations between ssGSEA scores (from full transcriptomic signal) of key HGSC signatures, and cell type fractions in stromal AOIs

correlation

Macrophages IFNg response is significantly higher in AOIs with high CD8 T-cells and DCs fractions, while p53, androgen response and IL2-STAT5 signatures are higher when these cell types are less abundant. B-cells MYC targets is significantly higher in AOIs with higher proportion of DCs and lower proportion of macrophages. Other macrophage signatures (MHC-II, IL6-JAK-STAT3 and TNFa) also change with differing DCs proportion.
